template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — SETTLEMENT DISTRIBUTION / CLOSING STATEMENT (PI)**:
A structured client-facing statement itemizing the settlement, attorneys' fees, costs, lien resolutions, and net to client. Required at the conclusion of every contingent-fee personal-injury matter under ABA Model Rule 1.5(c)(3) and most state analogs. Format: title block plus numbered sections, with a final client signature & acknowledgment. Target length: 2–4 pages depending on lien complexity.

**DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR — READ documentOptions**:
- `feeMode`: `flat_percentage` (default) | `tiered_pre_post_suit_post_trial` (e.g., 33⅓% pre-suit / 40% post-filing / 45% post-trial)
- `feeBase`: `net` (default — fee calculated on gross less costs, per ABA Model Rule 1.5(c) commentary) | `gross` — must match the controlling fee agreement
- `includeCostsItemization`: boolean (default true)
- `lienCategories`: array of any of `health_insurance`, `ERISA`, `Medicare`, `Medicaid`, `VA`, `TRICARE`, `hospital_lien`, `provider_LOP`, `child_support`, `other_subrogation` — emit a sub-line per active category
- `includeMedicareSetAside`: boolean (default false — case-by-case for future medicals where Medicare is interested party)
- `includeStructuredSettlement`: boolean (default false)
- `includeMinorCompromiseLanguage`: boolean (default false — required only when client is a minor or incapacitated person)
- `subPractice`: `mva` | `premises` | `med_mal` | `workers_comp_third_party` | `product_liability` | (omit for general)

**STRUCTURE** (follow this order; suppress sections whose option is disabled):

### Top of document
- Title banner (centered, bold, all caps): `SETTLEMENT DISTRIBUTION STATEMENT`
- Sub-banner (centered, italic): `Personal Injury Matter — Final Disbursement`
- Header block (left-aligned key/value pairs):
   Client:                [client name]
   Matter / File No.:     [firm file number]
   Date of Incident:      [date of incident]
   Date of Settlement:    [settlement date]
   Settling Party:        [defendant name + carrier]
   Carrier:               [carrier name]
   Claim No.:             [claim number]
   Counsel of Record:     [attorney name + firm]

### Section 1 — Gross Settlement
One line: `Gross Settlement Amount: $______`

### Section 2 — Attorneys' Fees
State the fee mode (`flat_percentage` or `tiered_pre_post_suit_post_trial`) and the fee base (`net` per ABA Model Rule 1.5(c)(3) default, OR `gross` if the fee agreement specifies). Show calculation:
- For `flat_percentage`: `Contingency Fee at [X]% of [net | gross]: $______`
- For `tiered_pre_post_suit_post_trial`: itemize each phase that applies (e.g., `33⅓% on $X (pre-suit portion)`, `40% on $Y (post-filing portion)`).
Cross-reference: 'Calculated per Section [X] of the executed Contingent Fee Agreement dated [date].'

### Section 3 — Costs and Expenses Advanced by Firm [suppress when includeCostsItemization=false]
Itemize costs (each line: provider/vendor, date, amount). Common entries:
- Filing fees
- Service-of-process fees
- Deposition costs (court reporter / videographer)
- Expert / consulting fees
- Medical-records charges
- Mediation / arbitration fees
- Travel
- Exhibit production
- Other (specify)
Sub-total: `Total Costs Advanced: $______`

### Section 4 — Liens and Third-Party Payouts
Emit one sub-line per active category in `lienCategories`. For each, show payee, statutory or contractual basis (cite where applicable), gross amount asserted, reduction negotiated (if any), and final amount paid:

- **Health-insurance / ERISA subrogation** (`health_insurance` or `ERISA`): payee + statutory/contractual basis. ERISA: cite 29 U.S.C. § 1132(a)(3) and Sereboff v. Mid Atlantic Medical Servs., 547 U.S. 356 (2006) for equitable-tracing limits.
- **Medicare conditional payments** (`Medicare`): cite 42 U.S.C. § 1395y(b)(2) (Medicare Secondary Payer) and reference the MSPRC final demand letter (attach if available).
- **Medicaid liens** (`Medicaid`): cite Arkansas Dep't of Health & Human Servs. v. Ahlborn, 547 U.S. 268 (2006) for proportional allocation; cite the state Medicaid third-party-recovery statute via JURISDICTION CONTEXT (e.g., 62 P.S. § 1409 in PA; N.J.S.A. 30:4D-7.1 in NJ; M.G.L. c. 118E § 22 in MA).
- **Hospital / provider statutory liens** (`hospital_lien`): cite the state lien statute via JURISDICTION CONTEXT (e.g., O.C.G.A. § 44-14-470 in GA; 770 ILCS 23 in IL; Cal. Civ. Code § 3045.1 in CA).
- **Provider LOPs** (`provider_LOP`): one line per LOP-holding provider, referencing the executed Letter of Protection.
- **Child-support arrears intercept** (`child_support`): per applicable state intercept statute (cite via JURISDICTION CONTEXT).
- **Other subrogation** (`other_subrogation`): describe the subrogor, basis, and amount.

Sub-total: `Total Lien & Third-Party Payouts: $______`

### Section 5 — Special Needs Trust / Medicare Set-Aside [suppress unless includeMedicareSetAside=true OR includeStructuredSettlement=true]
One paragraph summarizing any Medicare Set-Aside (MSA) allocation under CMS's Workers' Compensation MSA review process (note: liability MSAs remain unsettled — the attorney must verify case-specific posture). When `includeStructuredSettlement=true`, summarize the structured payment schedule, custodian, and tax framework.

### Section 6 — Net to Client
One calculated line: `Net Disbursement to Client: $______`
Followed by a formula recap: `Net = Gross − (Attorneys' Fees + Costs Advanced + Total Liens & Third-Party Payouts) [± any MSA / Structured Settlement adjustments].`

### Section 7 — Tax Disclosure (Informational)
One short paragraph: 'Recoveries on account of physical personal injuries are generally excluded from gross income under 26 U.S.C. § 104(a)(2). Punitive damages and pre-judgment interest may be taxable. This statement does not constitute tax advice; the client should consult an independent tax advisor.'

### Section 8 — Client Acknowledgment and Approval
One paragraph stating: 'Client has reviewed each line of this Settlement Distribution Statement, has had an opportunity to ask questions, and approves the disbursement as itemized above.' Followed by signature lines:

`Client Signature: ______________________   Date: __________
[Print client name]`

### Section 9 — Counsel Certification
One short paragraph: 'The undersigned counsel certifies that the above disbursement is consistent with the executed Contingent Fee Agreement, applicable rules of professional conduct (ABA Model Rule 1.5(c)(3) and the analogous state rule), and the firm's trust-account obligations under ABA Model Rule 1.15.'
Followed by:

`Attorney Signature: ______________________   Date: __________
[Print attorney name + bar number]`

### Section 10 — Minor / Incapacitated-Person Compromise [include only when includeMinorCompromiseLanguage=true]
One paragraph referencing the court-approved minor compromise / structured-settlement / blocked-account / guardianship plan for the funds, citing the state minor-compromise statute or rule via JURISDICTION CONTEXT (e.g., R. 4:44 in NJ; Pa.R.C.P. No. 2039 in PA; O.C.G.A. § 29-3-3 in GA; Fla. Stat. § 744.387 in FL). Reference the relevant court order.

**CONSTRAINTS**:
- Do NOT compute or assert specific dollar amounts — leave `$______` for the implementer to fill in. The template generates the structure, NOT the numbers.
- Do NOT cite a specific state Medicaid or hospital-lien statute unless JURISDICTION CONTEXT supplies it — use placeholders (e.g., `[STATE MEDICAID THIRD-PARTY RECOVERY STATUTE]`).
- Do NOT include the client's full Social Security Number on this statement.
- For any disabled `lienCategories` entry, suppress that bullet entirely.
- This statement is NOT a release or settlement agreement — see `settlement_release_pi_*` for release language.
- Total length: 2–4 pages depending on lien complexity.
hints: Settlement Distribution / Closing Statement for contingent-fee PI matters. Required at conclusion of representation under ABA Model Rule 1.5(c)(3). Itemizes gross, attorneys' fees, costs, liens (health insurance / ERISA / Medicare / Medicaid / hospital / LOP / child-support), and net to client. Not a release — separate settlement_release_pi_* templates for release language. Cite Ahlborn for Medicaid allocation, 42 U.S.C. § 1395y(b)(2) for Medicare Secondary Payer, 26 U.S.C. § 104(a)(2) for tax exclusion, ABA Model Rule 1.5(c)(3) and 1.15. State-specific hospital and Medicaid lien statutes deferred to JURISDICTION CONTEXT. 2–4 pages.

verificationSources.citation: Arkansas Dep't of Health & Human Servs. v. Ahlborn, 547 U.S. 268 (2006)
Sereboff v. Mid Atlantic Medical Servs., 547 U.S. 356 (2006)

knownLimitations: Generic — does not include state-specific hospital-lien or Medicaid-lien statutes; state-specific siblings or JURISDICTION CONTEXT must supply citations (e.g., O.C.G.A. § 44-14-470 in GA; 770 ILCS 23 in IL; M.G.L. c. 118E § 22 in MA; 62 P.S. § 1409 in PA; N.J.S.A. 30:4D-7.1 in NJ).
Medicare Secondary Payer compliance is fact-specific; MSA decisions in liability cases remain unsettled — a separate MSA template may be needed for high-value future-medical claims.

Minor-compromise language is jurisdiction-specific (court approval, GAL, structured settlement, blocked accounts) — placeholder included; state variant or attorney edit required (e.g., R. 4:44 in NJ; Pa.R.C.P. No. 2039 in PA; O.C.G.A. § 29-3-3 in GA; Fla. Stat. § 744.387 in FL).
Tax disclosure is informational only and does not substitute for tax advice — the attorney must direct client to independent tax counsel.
Statement is NOT a release or settlement agreement — see settlement_release_pi_* templates for release language.
ERISA equitable-tracing limits under Sereboff and US Airways v. McCutchen depend on specific plan-document language — the attorney must read the plan before stipulating to ERISA claim amounts.
